name: Boltzmann entropy and the second law of thermodynamics
principles: statistical foundation of the second law
entropy as logarithm of microstate count
conventions: W is the multiplicity: number of microstates compatible with a given macrostate (energy, volume, particle number)
k_B = 1.380649 x 10^-23 J/K (exact, defined value since 2019 SI redefinition)
entropy of the universe = system + surroundings; only the total is constrained by the second law
differential form for thermodynamic entropy of a closed system: dS >= delta Q / T, with equality for reversible processes
for ideal gases the Sackur-Tetrode equation gives S(N, V, U) explicitly
absolute zero of entropy fixed by the third law: S -> 0 as T -> 0 for a perfect crystal (W = 1, ln W = 0)
expectedLimits: reversible process in an isolated system: dS = 0; entropy is a constant of the motion in the strict reversible limit
irreversible process in an isolated system: dS > 0; equality only at thermodynamic equilibrium
two systems in thermal contact: heat flows from higher T to lower T until temperatures equalise (entropy maximum)
free expansion of an ideal gas (V_1 -> V_2): Delta S = N k_B ln(V_2/V_1) > 0
Boltzmann H-theorem (kinetic theory): -H functional decreases under collisions, microscopic justification of second law
references: Boltzmann (1877) — Über die Beziehung zwischen dem zweiten Hauptsatze...
Gibbs (1902) — Elementary Principles in Statistical Mechanics
Callen — Thermodynamics and an Introduction to Thermostatistics, ch.4
Reif — Fundamentals of Statistical and Thermal Physics, ch.3-4
BIPM — SI Brochure 9th ed. (defining value of k_B)
